inline_comments.message: I think this has been declared earlier in the interface, if so should be marked as @Override to avoid sonars.

retrieving the policy-type by parsing the policyName is an error prone practice because there are no restrictions on the intrinsic policy-name values that allows having the "_" and "." inside any part of the name.

For instance, if I have policy with policyName = DCAE_alex.Config_alex_was_here.5.xml
this parsing will return "Config_alex_was" - instead of "Config"

Another example is DCAE_alex.Config_alex_was.here_today.5.xml that will return "here" - totally not related piece of policyName...

Sorry :-(

this is much better !!!

- the only concern left is if I create a "Base" type policy with the name that starts with pieces like "MS_".  

For instance, /createPolicy policyName=DCAE_alex.MS_alex that is converted by PAP to DCAE_alex.Config_MS_alex.5.xml.  In this case this function will return "MS" type instead of the "Base"...

I guess we will have to live with this
